verses.text: Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help, trusting horses and chariots, 'but they look not unto the Holy One of Israel.' The Egyptians are men, not God. To lean on the strong arm of the world while ignoring God is fatal folly.

Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help, trusting horses and chariots, 'but they look not unto the Holy One of Israel.' The Egyptians are men, not God. To lean on the strong arm of the world while ignoring God is fatal folly.

Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help, trusting horses and chariots, 'but they look not unto the Holy One of Israel.' The Egyptians are men, not God. To lean on the strong arm of the world while ignoring God is fatal folly.

As a lion is not frightened from its prey, so the LORD will come down to fight for Zion, and 'as birds flying' He will defend Jerusalem. His protection is sure. God hovers over His people to shield and deliver them.

As a lion is not frightened from its prey, so the LORD will come down to fight for Zion, and 'as birds flying' He will defend Jerusalem. His protection is sure. God hovers over His people to shield and deliver them.

Turn to Him from whom you have deeply revolted; then men shall cast away their idols, and 'the Assyrian shall fall with the sword, not of a mighty man.' Repentance precedes rescue. God overthrows the foe by His own hand, not man's.
